title: Clinical Guidance for Influenza Vaccination | Influenza (Flu) | CDC
text:
en you get a flu shot while pregnant or breastfeeding, your body develops antibodies against flu that are shared with your baby through breast milk. 
Keep Reading 
Influenza Vaccine Safety Considerations during Pregnancy or while Breastfeeding 
Clinical Guidance for Seasonal Influenza Vaccine Safety 
CDC and FDA monitor the safety of all vaccines licensed in the United States, including seasonal influenza vaccines. Studies support the safety of annual influenza vaccination in children and adults.

Influenza (Flu) 
Influenza (flu) is a contagious respiratory illness caused by influenza viruses that infect the nose, throat, and lungs.
